mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes Viano 3.2 or 3.7 petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M112 V6 petrol engine typically costs £4,000–£6,500 supply only depending on which engine you have. Used engines are available from £2,000–£4,000. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800 depending on your location and workshop. Main dealer new engines are no longer available — the M112 is out of production.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes Viano 3.2 or 3.7 petrol engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M112 V6 is widely regarded as one of Mercedes' most durable petrol engines. With proper maintenance, these engines easily reach 400,000 to 500,000 kilometres. However, they have known age-related issues: ignition coil failures, MAF sensor problems, crankshaft pulley deterioration, and oil leaks from valve cover breathers. The main drawback is high fuel consumption of 11–13 litres per 100 km.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes Viano 3.2 and 3.7 petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are ignition coil and MAF sensor failures (typical V6 weaknesses), crankshaft pulley rubber disintegration (the signature failure of the M112 series), and oil leaks from valve cover breathers and gaskets. The engines are generally very durable and major failures are rare.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes Viano petrol engine, or should I scrap the van?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £8,000 Viano 3.7, a £5,500 recon is 69% — approaching our 70% Mercedes threshold. Against a tired £4,000 example, the same recon exceeds the threshold — get a scrap valuation first. Well-maintained Viano petrols are becoming rarer — a proper restoration is often justified for a clean example.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes Viano petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. 3.2 V6 (2003–2010): M112.951. 3.7 V6 (2004–2010): M112.976. The badge on the tailgate is the easiest tell — '3.2' or '3.7'.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the ignition coil issues on the Viano petrol V6, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — ignition coil replacement is a straightforward repair costing £200–£800. Viano petrol V6 engines are known for occasional problems with ignition coils, but they generally remain very durable. If you experience misfires or rough running, have the ignition system diagnosed. Replacing faulty coils is a simple fix, not an engine replacement issue.

eraMap.title: The VIANO 3.2 / 3.7 PETROL THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: The original Viano V6 — 218 hp, 305 Nm. One of Mercedes' most durable petrol V6s. High fuel consumption is the main drawback.
The larger V6 — 231 hp, 340 Nm. More power, same legendary M112 durability.

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes Viano 3.2 / 3.7 Petrol Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: No longer available from Mercedes — the M112 is out of production

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the M112 3.2 and 3.7-litre V6 petrol engines. Reconditioned M112 engines are available from specialist suppliers. Main dealer new engines are no longer available.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Viano 3.2/3.7 petrol engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent) or £120–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The V6's packaging in the engine bay makes this a moderately complex job.

cta.label: → Compare Viano petrol engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Ignition Coil & MAF Sensor Failures
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfires, engine warning light, loss of power, hesitation on acceleration, poor fuel economy.
problems.repairCost: £200–£800 (ignition coil and/or MAF sensor replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £4,000–£7,500 (if misfires lead to catalytic converter damage)
problems.recommendation: Viano petrol V6 engines are known for occasional problems with ignition coils and mass air flow (MAF) sensors. If you experience rough running or misfires, have the ignition system and MAF sensor diagnosed. Ignition coil failures are a typical V6 weakness, but generally the engines remain very durable. Replacing faulty coils or sensors is a straightforward repair.

failureLink.label: Read the full ignition system failure guide

problems.title: Crankshaft Pulley Deterioration (Crank Pulley Rubber Disintegration)
problems.symptoms: Engine vibration, squealing noises from the front of the engine, potential loss of power steering or alternator function, unusual engine noises.
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (crankshaft pulley replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £4,000–£7,500 (if left untreated leads to engine damage)
problems.recommendation: The signature failure of the M112 engine series is the destruction of the crankshaft pulley. The rubber in the crank pulley disintegrates over time. If you notice unusual engine vibration or squealing noises, have the crankshaft pulley inspected promptly. Replacement is relatively inexpensive compared to the cost of engine damage if the pulley fails completely.

failureLink.label: Read the full crankshaft pulley failure guide

problems.title: Oil Leaks — Valve Cover Breathers & Gaskets
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the car, burning oil smell from the engine bay, low oil level warning, visible oil residue around the valve covers.
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (valve cover gasket and breather reseal)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £4,000–£7,500 (if oil loss leads to engine damage)
problems.recommendation: The valve cover breathers (the PCV system of this engine) always leak after a while. Due to contamination of the crankcase ventilation, oil creeps out from under the gaskets and seals. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the valve cover gaskets and breathers inspected. Resealing with proper sealant is an effective solution.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Viano 3.2 / 3.7 Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Uncommon but possible on very high-mileage examples
Typically requires full engine replacement or complete rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Viano Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Mercedes service history with evidence of regular oil changes
* No misfires or rough running (ignition system health)
* No oil leaks (valve cover breathers and gaskets)
* No unusual engine vibration or squealing (crankshaft pulley health)
* Well-maintained M112 engines are known to exceed 400,000–500,000 kilometres
avoidIf: * Misfires with no documented ignition system work
* Oil leaks with no documented gasket replacement history
* Unusual engine vibration or squealing (crankshaft pulley warning)
* Poor service history — the M112 is durable but needs good maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Viano 3.2 and 3.7 Petrol are powered by Mercedes' M112 V6 — widely regarded as one of the most durable petrol engines ever built by Mercedes. With proper maintenance, these engines easily reach 400,000 to 500,000 kilometres. The main drawbacks are high fuel consumption (11–13 litres per 100 km) and age-related issues: ignition coil failures, MAF sensor problems, crankshaft pulley deterioration, and oil leaks from valve cover breathers. The 3.7-litre offers more power with similar reliability. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained Viano petrol is a £5,000–£15,000 premium MPV; a neglected one is a £5,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare Viano petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes Viano 3.2 or 3.7 Petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for smooth idle; any misfires or rough running? (ignition system warning)
* Service History — including which engine (3.2 M112.951 or 3.7 M112.976) and confirmed oil-change intervals
* Ignition Check — Any history of misfires, rough idle, or ignition coil replacements? (common V6 weakness)
* Crankshaft Pulley Check — Any vibration, squealing, or unusual noises from the front of the engine?
* Oil Leak Check — Check for oil spots under the car or visible residue around valve covers
* MAF Sensor Check — Any hesitation, loss of power, or poor fuel economy?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved ignition, MAF, or sensor issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
